key_insight: MADA-RL boosts compact-model reasoning by training specialized generator and critic agents with a counterfactual, debate-aware advantage that rewards critics for outperforming generator consensus.
engineering_relevance: It offers a parameter-efficient post-training recipe for edge- and budget-constrained reasoning systems, improving accuracy with LoRA-only fine-tuning rather than full-model updates.
method: Split one compact model into generator and critic roles.
Train with a counterfactual critic advantage: critic reward minus generator ensemble accuracy.
Deploy the agents in a lightweight multi-round debate protocol with LoRA adapters only.
evidence_signal: On five math benchmarks, DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-1.5B improved from 39.9% to 41.9% (+2.0 points, p < 0.001) with 16× fewer trainable parameters than full fine-tuning.
watch_item: The gains do not surpass the strongest larger-data baselines, and the multi-round inference protocol adds deployment-time cost.
paper_title: MADA-RL: Multi-Agent Debate-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Parameter-Efficient Reasoning in Compact Models
